title: Setting `--platform` or `--variant` in `podman build` does not clear the variant of layers
body:
### Issue Description

When building an image for `linux/amd64` on modern macOS machine (`darwin/arm64`) via `podman build --platform linux/amd64 [...]` or `podman build --os linux --arch amd64 [...]` or `podman build --os linux --arch amd64 --variant "" [...]`, pulling or running the resulting image on a `linux/amd64` machine still displays the following error message:

```
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64/v8) does not match the expected platform (linux/amd64)
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On a macOS arm64 machine, run `printf "FROM scratch\nUSER 12345\n" | podman build . -t test-image:latest --platform linux/amd64 -f -`
2. Push `test-image:latest` to a registry of choice.


### Describe the results you received

Pull `test-image:latest` on a `linux/amd64` machine. Then attempt `podman run --rm test-image:latest` (replace image name as necessary for how you have tagged the test image).

```
$ podman run --rm test-image:latest
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64/v8) does not match the expected platform (linux/amd64)
Error: crun: cannot find `` in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

The error is expected (there is no shell in the test image), but the warning is not expected.

In addition, we can save the image with `podman save test-image:latest > image.tar`. Then unpack the tar file and open the json file has `variant` still set to `v8`:

```
{"created":"2025-06-05T04:58:51.440945534Z","architecture":"amd64","os":"linux","variant":"v8","config":{"User":"12345","Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"WorkingDir":"/","Labels":{"io.buildah.version":"1.40.0"}},"rootfs":{"type":"layers","diff_ids":[]},"history":[{"created":"2025-06-05T04:58:51.440976826Z","created_by":"/bin/sh -c #(nop) USER 12345","empty_layer":true}]}
```

### Describe the results you expected

The warning should not appear when running the image, and the `variant` field in the json file should be empty, as we requested at image build time.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```console
$ sw_vers
ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		15.5
BuildVersion:		24F74
```

```console
$ system_profiler SPHardwareDataType
Hardware:

    Hardware Overview:

      Model Name: MacBook Pro
      Model Identifier: Mac16,8
      Model Number: Z1FC000EQLL/A
      Chip: Apple M4 Pro
      Total Number of Cores: 14 (10 performance and 4 efficiency)
      Memory: 48 GB
      System Firmware Version: 11881.121.1
      OS Loader Version: 11881.121.1
      Serial Number (system): <redacted>
      Hardware UUID: <redacted>
      Provisioning UDID: <redacted>
      Activation Lock Status: Disabled
```

### Additional information

In the process of investigating, I looked into https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22730 but that seems to affect manifest lists; this case occurs with building a single image for a platform which differs from the host.

comments.body: Please test with podman v5.5.1 again, I think this is a dup of github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6185

Thanks, the issue does indeed appear to be fixed now. For anyone else running into this, note that I had to upgrade both the `podman` CLI and also the podman machine in order to receive the buildah fix:

```console
$ podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 41.20250215.3.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Fri Jun  6 09:41:32 2025 from 192.168.127.1
core@localhost:~$ sudo bootc upgrade
layers already present: 51; layers needed: 19 (167.0 MB)
Fetched layers: 159.22 MiB in 28 seconds (5.63 MiB/s)
  Deploying: done (1 second)
Queued for next boot: quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.5
  Version: 41.20250215.3.0
  Digest: sha256:210d3f72e0c0fd26b9c85f140f82ba3a35604614f6a1931c2f0470d81561bfd6
Total new layers: 70    Size: 915.7 MB
Removed layers:   19    Size: 143.8 MB
Added layers:     19    Size: 167.0 MB
core@localhost:~$ exit
logout
$ podman machine stop && podman machine start
```
